name: Cranberry Pinwheels
recipeInstructions:
In a large bowl, cream together the butter and granulated sugar until light and fluffy. Add the egg and vanilla, mixing well.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, salt, ground cranberries, walnuts, grated orange peel, and brown sugar. Gradually add this mixture to the butter mixture, stirring until just combined.
Cover the dough with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 2 hours.
In a small bowl, mix the remaining brown sugar and milk.
Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease two cookie sheets.
Roll out the chilled dough on a lightly floured surface to form a large rectangle. Spread the brown sugar mixture evenly over the dough.
Sprinkle the ground cranberries, walnuts, and grated orange peel over the dough, leaving a quarter-inch border along the narrow ends.
Starting at one of the narrow ends, roll the dough tightly into a jelly roll. Wrap the roll in wax paper, seam side down, and freeze overnight.
Cut the frozen roll into 1/4 inch slices, spacing them about three inches apart on the prepared cookie sheets.
Bake for 12 to 15 minutes or until the edges are lightly browned. Remove from the oven and cool on a wire rack.
